int64_weights.summary: INT64 tensors detected in graph constants or weights.
int64_weights.explanation: TensorRT does not natively support INT64. Engine build will cast to INT32, which can overflow for large indices.
int64_weights.remediation: Before exporting, cast indices to torch.int32: e.g. `idx = idx.to(torch.int32)`. For embedding lookups, ensure the indices tensor is int32.

int64_input.explanation: TensorRT does not natively support INT64. Engine build will cast INT64 inputs to INT32, which can overflow for large index values.
int64_input.remediation: Cast the input to int32 before exporting, or feed int32 tensors at inference time. For embedding/gather indices, ensure they are int32.

uint8_input.explanation: TensorRT inputs must be FLOAT32, FLOAT16, INT32, or INT8. UINT8 image tensors are common in preprocessing but must be cast before reaching the model.
uint8_input.remediation: Apply the UINT8 -> FLOAT32 conversion (and normalization) in your preprocessing pipeline, not inside the exported model.

bf16_unsupported.explanation: BF16 is supported only on Ampere+ GPUs and TRT 8.6+. On older targets the build fails or silently promotes to FP32.
bf16_unsupported.remediation: If targeting older GPUs or TRT 8.0, export the model in FP16 instead of BF16.

float64_tensors.explanation: TensorRT does not support double precision (FLOAT64). Models trained or exported with float64 must be downcast before conversion.
float64_tensors.remediation: Call `model = model.float()` before torch.onnx.export, and verify with `onnx.checker` that no FLOAT64 remains.

string_tensors.explanation: TensorRT has no string type. Models that process raw strings (tokenizers, label encoders) cannot be deployed end-to-end with TRT.
string_tensors.remediation: Move string preprocessing out of the model and pass integer token IDs instead.

fully_dynamic_input_shape.summary: Input tensor has every dimension dynamic.
fully_dynamic_input_shape.explanation: TRT can build dynamic engines, but optimizer cannot estimate memory or fuse layers well when every dimension is symbolic.
fully_dynamic_input_shape.remediation: Pin spatial dims at export: pass dynamic_axes={'input': {0: 'batch'}} (or whatever truly varies) and leave H,W concrete.

missing_output.explanation: TensorRT refuses to build engines for graphs with zero outputs. Typically caused by aggressive constant-folding or shape inference failure.
missing_output.remediation: Inspect with `onnx.checker.check_model`. Re-export with do_constant_folding=False to localize the issue.

isolated_node.explanation: Disconnected subgraphs are silently dropped by TRT, which sometimes hides bugs in the exported graph (e.g. an inplace side effect that ONNX could not express).
isolated_node.remediation: Confirm the orphan node represents intentional dead code. If not, audit the forward() method for unsupported in-place ops.

duplicate_node_name.summary: Multiple nodes share the same name.
duplicate_node_name.explanation: ONNX permits non-unique node names but several TRT tooling paths (engine introspection, layer-precision overrides) assume uniqueness.
duplicate_node_name.remediation: Set unique node names during export, or run an ONNX simplifier pass before TRT conversion.

loop_dynamic_trip_count.summary: Loop op with non-constant trip count.
loop_dynamic_trip_count.explanation: TensorRT requires a statically known or shape-inferable trip count for Loop. Dynamic trip counts derived from runtime tensor values fail at build time.
loop_dynamic_trip_count.remediation: Refactor the loop to use a fixed iteration count. If the count depends on input size, consider unrolling or a fully-vectorized formulation.

nested_loop.explanation: TensorRT does not support Loop-within-Loop. Inner loops must be unrolled or flattened.
nested_loop.remediation: Restructure the model to use a single Loop with combined trip count, or fully unroll inner iterations.

if_branch_shape_mismatch.summary: If branches produce outputs with incompatible shapes or dtypes.
if_branch_shape_mismatch.explanation: TRT requires both then/else branches to emit identically shaped, identically typed tensors.
if_branch_shape_mismatch.remediation: Make the two branches produce the same output shape; pad or broadcast the smaller branch to match.

if_detected_unverified.summary: If node detected; branch compatibility not verified.
if_detected_unverified.explanation: TRT requires both then/else branches to emit identically shaped, identically typed tensors. trtcheck cannot confirm this without full shape inference, so every If is flagged for manual review.
if_detected_unverified.remediation: Verify both branches return tensors of matching shape and dtype. If they differ, pad or broadcast the smaller branch to match before exporting.

scan_dynamic_length.summary: Scan op with dynamic sequence length.
scan_dynamic_length.explanation: TRT requires Scan sequence length to be known at engine build time.
scan_dynamic_length.remediation: Fix the scan length at export, or replace with a Loop over a constant range.

unsupported_operator.explanation: This op type has no native TensorRT layer. Engine build will fail.
unsupported_operator.remediation: Replace with an equivalent supported op, write a TRT plugin, or remove from the graph if it is dead.

partial_operator.explanation: TRT supports this op only for certain attribute or dtype combinations.
partial_operator.remediation: Check the operator-specific notes in the report for the exact restriction.

large_constant.explanation: TRT will lift large constants into engine weights, which can balloon engine file size if the constant is per-batch data instead of a learned weight.
large_constant.remediation: Verify the constant is genuinely a learned weight rather than e.g. a baked-in image.

opset_too_old.summary: ONNX opset version below recommended floor.
opset_too_old.explanation: TRT 10.x has best coverage for opset 17+; older opsets may force conservative decompositions.
opset_too_old.remediation: Re-export with opset_version=17 (or the highest your training framework supports).

external_data_missing.summary: Model references external weight files that were not found.
external_data_missing.explanation: ONNX supports storing weights in separate files. If they are missing at analysis time TRT will also fail to load them.
external_data_missing.remediation: Ensure all sibling .data files are present, or re-export with save_as_external_data=False.

input_with_no_type.summary: Graph input declares no tensor element type.
input_with_no_type.explanation: TRT requires every input to declare its element type.
input_with_no_type.remediation: Re-export the model; this typically indicates a corrupted ONNX file.

in_place_aliasing.summary: Heuristic detection of in-place aliasing artifacts.
in_place_aliasing.explanation: PyTorch in-place operations (e.g. relu_) sometimes survive ONNX export as redundant Identity chains, which can confuse TRT shape inference.
in_place_aliasing.remediation: Replace in-place activations with their out-of-place counterparts.
